runs.drain_imbalance: producer is ~10x faster than consumer; per-iteration sentinel never becomes visible within reasonable timeout
runs.alloc_profile_blocked_by: JMH iteration setUp() never completed (sendEmptyPushAndWait timeout) so async-profiler never reached the measurement window where flame graphs are emitted at JVM exit

conclusion.label: in_memory broker DROPS throughput; per-topic synchronized produce mutex becomes the new bottleneck
evidence: Run A (apache_kafka) produced 4 [E2E] lines (128754 / 130951 / 131292 / 130779 ops/sec) consistent with Phase 5/6/7 baseline (132-142k window)
Run B (in_memory) successfully replaced the in-process Apache Kafka broker; 3 InMemoryPubSubBroker instances were registered (parent + dc-0 + dc-1), each with both plaintext and SSL aliases
AaKafkaBrokerReporter and AaKafkaPipelineReporter emitted 'no Kafka MBeans available; reporter inactive' once each in Run B then went silent (no throws -- criterion 5 dependency met)
Run B's producer side runs vastly faster than Apache Kafka because MockInMemoryProducerAdapter.sendMessage() returns synchronously (no batching, no I/O, no broker request thread)
BUT: each invocation of the benchmark's runPutWorkload() returns in microseconds, so JMH calls @Benchmark thousands of times per warmup iteration, generating millions of records

Server-side consume rate is bounded by the synchronized poll on InMemoryPubSubTopic plus the AbstractPollStrategy.maxMessagePerPoll cap (raised from 3 to 500 in this phase, but still throttled by the synchronized monitor)

Aggregate per-server consume throughput observed in [BOTTLENECK-SUMMARY] ticks: 2.0M-2.4M records per 20s tick = 100-120k records/sec per server, comparable to Apache Kafka's 130k -- but the producer's input rate is ~10x higher
Net result: per-iteration sentinel never becomes visible within the teardown timeout; no [E2E] line is ever emitted; effective end-to-end throughput is 0 ops/sec
Even an empty push (single record from each region) does not complete within 5 minutes under the in-memory broker because the system-store creation chain serializes through the same per-topic monitor
conclusion.interpretation: The in-memory broker WOULD be much faster if the produce/consume locks were finer-grained (per-partition rather than per-topic). The current implementation's coarse-grained synchronized produce on InMemoryPubSubTopic effectively serializes all produces to a topic across all producers, and the symmetric synchronized consume serializes all polls. Under heavy concurrent load (Phase 5 winning config: WC=off, RMD cache=on, 100k keys, multi-producer N=2 from each of 2 regions, plus shared-consumer pools, plus per-server admin/version-topic consumers, plus bot HelixParticipationService), the producer side wins the lock contention contest because it has more threads and the consumer cannot keep up.
conclusion.phase7_revision: Phase 7's conclusion (b) ('no Kafka knob lifts the cap') is NOT revised by this experiment. We did not produce comparable [E2E] data from the in-memory path because the in-memory broker imposes its own (different, coarser) bottleneck. The right next step is to fix the in-memory broker first, OR to run a pure-leader micro-benchmark that bypasses the multi-region cluster wrapper entirely.
conclusion.fix_proposed_for_in_memory_broker: Change InMemoryPubSubTopic to hold a per-partition synchronized list (instead of a per-topic synchronized map of partitions). Specifically: replace `synchronized produce(...)` and `synchronized consume(...)` on InMemoryPubSubTopic with a `Lock[] partitionLocks = new ReentrantLock[partitionCount]` array, and lock only the relevant partition. This removes the all-producer contention point and lets producers from different (region, partition) tuples advance independently. Estimated implementation effort: half a day in InMemoryPubSubTopic.java, no API surface change required. Cross-cutting risk: the existing StoreIngestionTaskTest and the few dozen other unit tests that use MockInMemoryConsumerAdapter rely on the synchronized contract for ordering guarantees, so this fix needs careful test sweep; recommend Phase 9 (a) to take this on.

criteria.1_in_memory_broker_factory_added: PASS - InMemoryPubSubBrokerFactory.java has public no-arg constructor and implements PubSubBrokerFactory<InMemoryPubSubBrokerWrapper>
criteria.2_broker_wrapper_and_registry: PASS - 3 brokers registered in Run B (parent, dc-0, dc-1) under 6 addresses (each plaintext + SSL); registry lookup paths exercised by all factories at create() time
criteria.3_three_adapter_factories: PASS - InMemoryProducerAdapterFactory, InMemoryConsumerAdapterFactory, InMemoryAdminAdapterFactory each have public no-arg constructors and were instantiated reflectively by Venice's Class.forName path during Run B
criteria.4_benchmark_gating: PASS - ActiveActiveIngestionBenchmark.setUp() reads -Dvenice.benchmark.use.inmemory.pubsub (default false), sets pubSubBrokerFactory system property BEFORE the first ServiceFactory call, and echoes the choice via System.err. Both Run A and Run B logs contain the expected echo
criteria.5_two_canonical_runs: PARTIAL - Run A completed within +/-10% of Phase 7 baseline (130865 ops/s vs 132-142k window: 1% below the lower edge of the window, well within JMH variance for a 4-iteration run); Run B never emitted an [E2E] line because the synchronized in-memory broker became the new cap; AaKafkaBrokerReporter and AaKafkaPipelineReporter were graceful no-throws under in-memory (single inactivity line per reporter, then silence)
criteria.6_allocation_profile_delta: FAIL - Run B's alloc flame graph could not be produced because no JMH measurement iteration completed (async-profiler emits flames at JVM exit, but JMH does not exit until iterations finish). The Phase 7 alloc profile (apache_kafka) is preserved in aa-profile-alloc/. The empty Run B alloc dir (aa-profile-alloc-inmemory/) reflects this.
criteria.7_result_table_and_json: PASS - aa-phase8-result.json (this file) and AA_INMEMORY_KAFKA_PHASE8_REPORT.md contain the comparison table; Run B's e2e_median_ops_per_sec is 0 (no completed iteration)
criteria.8_named_conclusion: PASS - conclusion (c): in_memory broker drops throughput, per-topic synchronized mutex is the new bottleneck; finer-grained per-partition locking is proposed as the fix for Phase 9
criteria.9_existing_tests_pass: PASS - ActiveActiveReplicationForHybridTest: tests=11 failures=0 errors=0; TestActiveActiveIngestion: tests=5 failures=0 errors=0; SITWithPWiseWithoutBufferAfterLeaderTest (concrete StoreIngestionTaskTest subclass): tests=318 failures=0 errors=0
criteria.10_artifacts: PASS - aa-phase8-result.json + AA_INMEMORY_KAFKA_PHASE8_REPORT.md are non-empty; report contains delta tables, conclusion, recommendation, and known-limitations sections

summary.blocking_failure: criterion 6 (alloc profile) -- gated on Run B completing at least one measurement iteration, which the synchronized in-memory broker prevents under canonical Phase 5 config
